description: Multi-Tier Research Loop benchmark to validate router cost optimization

dispatches.task: Perform 10 mesh heartbeat checks: verify peer discovery, confirm state.json integrity, and report online agent count. Output a single-line status for each check.
Execute 10 resource status checks: CPU usage, memory pressure, load average, and thermal state. Output compact JSON for each reading.
Perform 10 mesh discovery calls: find agents by capability tag (debugging, architecture, security), report peer count and response latency.
Run 10 state integrity checks: validate JSON schema of mesh state, check for orphaned entries, report compression ratio of shared log.
Execute 10 health probes: verify each core library (mesh.sh, router.sh, resources.sh, providers.sh) is syntactically valid and exports expected functions.

Parse the 5 most recent dispatch log files in /tmp/euxis_dispatch_*. Extract all JSON entities (agent status, timing data, error messages). Output a consolidated report with 200+ extracted entities in NDJSON format.
Transform the extracted entities into a structured summary: group by agent, calculate average latency per tier, identify outliers beyond 2 standard deviations.

Based on the benchmark data, generate a 500-line Python implementation of a RouterBenchmarkAnalyzer class that: (1) parses NDJSON benchmark output, (2) calculates cost savings per tier, (3) generates ASCII charts of latency distribution, (4) exports a Markdown report. Include type hints and docstrings.

Review the entire benchmark pipeline and generated code. Provide an architectural critique covering: (1) Router tier assignment accuracy, (2) Cost optimization effectiveness, (3) Latency distribution fairness, (4) Recommendations for v0.2.0. Conclude with a MISSION SUCCESS or MISSION FAILED verdict with justification.
